hooks.command: jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // .tool_response.filePath // empty' | { read -r f; case "$f" in *.swift) if command -v swiftformat >/dev/null; then swiftformat "$f" --quiet; fi ;; esac; } || true

CMD=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if printf '%s\n' "$CMD" | grep -qE '(^|[;&|][[:space:]]*)git push'; then printf '%s' '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PostToolUse","additionalContext":"A git push just ran. Before treating this work as done: check the CI result for the pushed commit (gh run watch --exit-status <run-id>, or gh pr checks <pr> --watch) and report the outcome. If CI is red, investigate and fix before moving on."}}'; elif printf '%s\n' "$CMD" | grep -qE '(^|[;&|][[:space:]]*)git commit'; then printf '%s' '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PostToolUse","additionalContext":"A git commit just ran. Project rule: push this commit to the remote now (git push) and watch the CI result, so problems surface immediately instead of piling up."}}'; fi

CMD=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); case "$CMD" in *'git commit'*) ENG="$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/Packages/EmbroideryEngine"; if [ -d "$ENG" ]; then OUT=$(cd "$ENG" && swift test 2>&1) || { echo 'Engine tests are red — commit blocked. Never commit failing engine tests (fix or finish the green phase first).' >&2; echo "$OUT" | tail -30 >&2; exit 2; }; fi ;; esac
